grounding_quotes: water vapor monitoring using the BDS PPP-B2b service
using ephemeris from the PPP-B2b service

experiment_epoch.description: 25-day continuously operated water vapor monitoring experiment in 2025, Wuhan
grounding_quotes: a continuously operated water vapor monitoring system in Wuhan, China

dcb_handling.description: Code bias corrections from BDS PPP-B2b service are used in the processing. Referred to as 'code bias corrections' rather than DCB explicitly.
grounding_quotes: satellite orbit, clock, and code bias corrections from the BDS PPP-B2b service

mathematical_model.processing_mode: Dual-frequency ionospheric-free PPP using code and phase observations from GPS and BDS
mathematical_model.ztd_to_pwv: Standard ZTD to PWV conversion using weighted mean temperature
grounding_quotes: Dual-frequency ionospheric-free PPP using code and phase observations from GPS and BDS

datasets.observation_data: GPS and BDS dual-frequency code and phase observations from Leica AR20 antenna and receiver
datasets.correction_source: BDS PPP-B2b real-time satellite orbit and clock corrections
grounding_quotes: Original observations from the Global Positioning System (GPS) and BDS are collected
PWV monitored with B2b ephemeris are evaluated with radiosonde and ERA5 reanalysis

pwv_accuracy.reference_note: Similar to CAS real-time ephemeris results

main_results.summary: BDS PPP-B2b service can support operational water vapor monitoring with PWV accuracy of 3.7-4.7mm RMS vs radiosonde. First demonstration that PPP-B2b-based water vapor monitoring meets operational meteorology requirements.
key_findings: Accuracy comparable to CAS real-time ephemeris results
PPP-B2b enables standalone water vapor monitoring in areas without internet
Meets basic demand for operational meteorology
grounding_quotes: RMS of PWV based on B2b ephemeris varies from 3.71 to 4.66 mm for different satellite combinations
this study demonstrates that the practical accuracy of water vapor monitored based on the BDS PPP-B2b service can meet the basic demand for operational meteorology for the first time

novelty_audit.rationale: First practical demonstration of BDS PPP-B2b for operational water vapor monitoring. Applies established PPP+PWV methodology to the new PPP-B2b correction source. Engineering transfer rather than algorithmic novelty.
grounding_quotes: this study demonstrates that the practical accuracy of water vapor monitored based on the BDS PPP-B2b service can meet the basic demand for operational meteorology for the first time

reproduction_blockers: 25-day GNSS observation dataset not publicly available
PPP-B2b correction stream for the experiment period not archived
PPP processing software configuration not disclosed in detail
No source code or processing workflow released
